#  TeleQKD line 
Single photon detectors for industrial Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) systems.
[ Discover more ](https://www.singlequantum.com/solutions/teleqkd-line/#section-number-3) [ Download datasheet ](https://www.singlequantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/SQ_TeleQKD_A4Brochure_v6-2024.pdf)
![](https://cdn.shortpixel.ai/spai/q_glossy+w_492+to_auto+ret_img/www.singlequantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/images-for-website-1-e1774443874289.png)
Go to
  * [ Introducing TeleQDK line ](https://www.singlequantum.com/solutions/teleqkd-line/#section-number-3)
  * [ Fiber Coupled ](https://www.singlequantum.com/solutions/teleqkd-line/#section-number-4)
  * [ Free-space coupled ](https://www.singlequantum.com/solutions/teleqkd-line/#section-number-5)
  * [ Datasheet ](https://www.singlequantum.com/solutions/teleqkd-line/#section-number-6)
  * [ Collaborations ](https://www.singlequantum.com/solutions/teleqkd-line/#section-number-7)
  * [ Scientific Publications ](https://www.singlequantum.com/solutions/teleqkd-line/#section-number-8)


Questions?
[ Talk to us ](https://www.singlequantum.com/contact/)
###### Key features
  * A cost-efficient but high quality solution
  * Optimized for 1550 nm
  * High efficiency & low noise
  * High count rate for high secret key rate
  * Compact and rack-mountable system
  * Automated cooldown and continuous operation
  * Low timing jitter and no after-pulsing
  * Low quantum bit error rate (QBER) maintained over long distance


## Introducing TeleQKD line: Our affordable high quality solution
How to create a QKD link from Amsterdam to Berlin? From QKD to quantum internet, Single Quantum provides the best solutions optimized for your communication network. Metropolitan areas could be connected with a distance of hundreds of kilometers, as demonstrated by Toshiba in 2017, 2019 and 2024 with a Single Quantum system.
[Read the article](https://www.nature.com/articles/d42473-022-00133-x)
Our rack-mountable SNSPD system alleviate potential bottlenecks in photon detection that limit communication networks, ensuring easy integration in research labs as well as industrial settings.
##  Fiber-coupled solution 
Our fiber-coupled system has been instrumental in advancing long-distance Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) technology across Europe and is trusted by leading industrial partners. Through a consortium with Q*Bird, we supported the longest single-fiber Measurement-Device-Independent Quantum Key Distribution (MDI-QKD) deployment and helped establish a key connection hub in the Port of Rotterdam as part of the world’s first scalable quantum internet. In collaboration with Toshiba, we contributed to extending QKD transmission distances beyond 300 km.
Together, these partnerships are driving the growth of secure quantum networks and pushing the boundaries of quantum communication
  * Rack-mountable cryostat (3U)
  * Compact and scalable electronic driver (2U)
  * Versatile and scalable system
  * Add-ons for best performances available


![](https://www.singlequantum.com/solutions/teleqkd-line/)
##  Free-space coupling solution 
Single Quantum provides a free-space coupling solution, Iris X. It comprises a cryostat with an optical window, which can host an SNSPD array perfect for free-space QKD as well as deep-space laser communication, as demonstrated in [our collaboration with ESA for Psyche Mission](https://www.esa.int/Enabling_Support/Operations/Shining_a_light_on_NASA_s_deep_space_demo?fbclid=IwAR1u_TSz9MoVV2Dspx7gQi0VyeMQMBKecOCJN4nVD7L03jGYVfT5efTvb5E).
  * Low noise environment for photon detection
  * Ample space allocated for auxiliary components
  * Capability to host an SNSPD array of up to 48 pixels


[Discover more](https://www.singlequantum.com/solutions/space-tech-line/)
![](https://www.singlequantum.com/solutions/teleqkd-line/)
#### Datasheet
[Download datasheet](https://www.singlequantum.com/solutions/teleqkd-line/)
##### How to keep your data safe with quantum technology?
One of the most promising approach to ensure a secure communication is Quantum Key Distribution (QKD), a quantum cryptography method to produce and share encryption keys. Moreover, quantum internet can enable sending and receiving quantum bits (qubits) over a large network, paving the way to cloud quantum computing.
Here, single photon detection is the key enabling technology. In particular, Single Quantum SNSPDs prove to be ideal for QKD and quantum internet applications, due to their outstanding performance [1,2,3].
#### Collaborations
##### **Collaborating to advance quantum communication**
At Single Quantum, we value collaboration as a cornerstone of innovation. By partnering with leading organizations and research groups, we bring together cutting-edge technologies to address the challenges of quantum communication. These collaborations enable us to push the boundaries of what’s possible, ensuring robust and scalable solutions for the growing demands of secure data transmission.

##### Toshiba Europe and Single Quantum parnter to probide extended long-distance QKD deployment capability
Toshiba Europe Ltd. and Single Quantum B.V. have collaborated to test and validate long-distance deployments of Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) technology.
Following extended validation testing of Toshiba’s QKD technology and Single Quantum’s superconducting nanowire single photon detectors (SNSPDs), both companies are pleased to announce a solution that substantially extends the transmission range for QKD deployment over fibre connections, up to and beyond 300km.
[Read the press release](https://www.singlequantum.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/Toshiba-Single-Quantum-PR-release_final-19.04-1.pdf)
####  Scientific Publications 
Quantum Communication 
  * [A comparative analysis of InGaAs SPADs and SNSPDs in entanglement-based Quantum communications](https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/abstract/document/10648193/keywords#keywords) (2024) – IEEE Explore
  * [Continuous entanglement distribution over a transnational 248 km fiber link](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41467-022-33919-0) (2022) – Nature Communication
  * [Photon-number entanglement generated by sequential excitation of a two-level atom](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41566-022-00979-z) (2022) – Nature Photonics
  * [600-km repeater-like quantum communications with dual-band stabilization ](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41566-021-00811-0)(2021) – Nature Photonics
  * [Two-photon quantum interference and entanglement at 2.1 μm](https://www.science.org/doi/10.1126/sciadv.aay5195) (2020) – Science Advances


Quantum Optics and Photonics research 
  * [Photon bound state dynamics from a single artificial atom ](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41567-023-01997-6)(2023) – Nature Physics
  * [High-rate entanglement between a semiconductor spin and indistinguishable photons](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41566-023-01186-0#further-reading) (2023) – Nature Photonics
  * [Electro-Optical Sampling of Single-Cycle THz Fields with Single-Photon Detectors](https://www.mdpi.com/1424-8220/22/23/9432) (2022) – Nature Communication
  * [Direct-bandgap emission from hexagonal Ge and SiGe alloys](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-020-2150-y) (2020) – Nature
  * [A gated quantum dot strongly coupled to an optical microcavity](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-019-1709-y) (2019) – Nature


Superconducting Single Photon Detectors 
  * [High-performance photon number resolving detectors for 850–950 nm wavelength range](https://pubs.aip.org/aip/app/article/9/6/066101/3296058/High-performance-photon-number-resolving-detectors) (2024) – APL Photonics
  * [Ultra-high system detection efficiency superconducting nanowire single-photon detectors for quantum photonics and life sciences](https://opg.optica.org/abstract.cfm?URI=CLEO_AT-2022-AW5P.2) (2022) – Optica CLEO Journal
  * [Efficient mid-infrared single-photon detection using superconducting NbTiN nanowires with high time resolution in a Gifford-McMahon cryocooler](https://opg.optica.org/prj/fulltext.cfm?uri=prj-10-4-1063&id=470770) (2022) – Optica Photonics Research
  * [Detecting telecom single photons with 99.5% system detection efficiency and high time resolution](https://pubs.aip.org/aip/app/article/6/3/036114/831362/Detecting-telecom-single-photons-with-99-5-2-07-0) (2021) – APL Photonics
  * [Multimode-fiber-coupled superconducting nanowire single-photon detectors with high detection efficiency and time resolution ](https://opg.optica.org/ao/fulltext.cfm?uri=ao-58-36-9803&id=423977)(2019) – Optica Applied Optics


##  Questions about the TeleQDK line? 
To tailor the optimal solution for you, our engineers will assist you, starting from understanding your needs and define the system specifications together with you.

                    <section id="section-number-3" class="section-number-3 section--bg-color s-content-with-sidebar">
    <div class="container">
        <div class="row">
            <div class="col-12 col-md-4 form--bg-dark sidebar fade slide-up">
                                                                                                                        <div class="c-content ">
        <h6>Key features</h6>
<ul class="list-one">
<li>A cost-efficient but high quality solution</li>
<li>Optimized for 1550 nm</li>
<li>High efficiency & low noise</li>
<li>High count rate for high secret key rate</li>
<li>Compact and rack-mountable system</li>
<li>Automated cooldown and continuous operation</li>
<li>Low timing jitter and no after-pulsing</li>
<li>Low quantum bit error rate (QBER) maintained over long distance</li>
</ul>

    </div>
                                                                                                                                                                                                                </div>
            <div class="col-12 col-md-7 content-right  fade slide-up">
                                                                                                        <div class="c-content ">
        <h2 class="heading-five">Introducing TeleQKD line: Our affordable high quality solution</h2>
<p>How to create a QKD link from Amsterdam to Berlin? From QKD to quantum internet, Single Quantum provides the best solutions optimized for your communication network. Metropolitan areas could be connected with a distance of hundreds of kilometers, as demonstrated by Toshiba in 2017, 2019 and 2024 with a Single Quantum system.</p>
<p><a class="button-one" href="https://www.nature.com/articles/d42473-022-00133-x" data-wpel-link="external" rel="external noopener noreferrer"><span class="button-link-label">Read the article</span></a></p>
<p>Our rack-mountable SNSPD system alleviate potential bottlenecks in photon detection that limit communication networks, ensuring easy integration in research labs as well as industrial settings.</p>

    </div>
                                                                                                                                                                                                                        </div>
        </div>
    </div>
</section>
                    <section id="section-number-4" class="section-number-4 s-content-media  ">
    <div class="container">
                <div class="row s-content-media__content-wrap  h-reverse-order--row ">

            <div class="col-12 col-md-5 fade slide-up  offset-lg-1  s-content-media__content-col  s-content-media__content-col--aligned-center ">

                <header class="c-header s-content-media__header">
                            
            <h2 class="c-heading c-header__heading heading-four ">
        Fiber-coupled solution

    </h2>
            </header>

                
                <div class="c-content  s-content-media__content">
        <p><span data-olk-copy-source="MessageBody">Our fiber-coupled system has been instrumental in advancing long-distance Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) technology across Europe and is trusted by leading industrial partners. Through a consortium with Q*Bird, we supported the longest single-fiber Measurement-Device-Independent Quantum Key Distribution (MDI-QKD) deployment and helped establish a key connection hub in the Port of Rotterdam as part of the world’s first scalable quantum internet. In collaboration with Toshiba, we contributed to extending QKD transmission distances beyond 300 km.</span></p>
<p>Together, these partnerships are driving the growth of secure quantum networks and pushing the boundaries of quantum communication</p>
<ul class="list-one">
<li>Rack-mountable cryostat (3U)</li>
<li>Compact and scalable electronic driver (2U)</li>
<li>Versatile and scalable system</li>
<li>Add-ons for best performances available</li>
</ul>

    </div>

                
                
            </div>
            <div class="col-12 col-md-6   s-content-media__image--aligned-center  s-content-media__image">
                    
                <div class="c-media s-media__media c-media--has-popup ">
    
    
    
            
        <div class="c-image c-media__image s-media__image none ">
    <img width="1875" height="1440" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZpZXdCb3g9IjAgMCAxODc1IDE0NDAiIHdpZHRoPSIxODc1IiBoZWlnaHQ9IjE0NDAiIGRhdGEtdT0iJTJGd3AtY29udGVudCUyRnVwbG9hZHMlMkYyMDI0JTJGMTAlMkZNYXNrLWdyb3VwLTMuanBnIiBkYXRhLXc9IjE4NzUiIGRhdGEtaD0iMTQ0MCIgZGF0YS1iaXA9IiI+PC9zdmc+" data-spai="1" class="c-image__img " alt="" decoding="async" data-spai-target="src" data-spai-orig="/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/Mask-group-3.jpg" data-spai-exclude="none" data-spai-orig-obs="true">
  </div>

        
            
    
    </div>
            </div>
        </div>
    </div>
</section>
                    <section id="section-number-5" class="section-number-5 s-content-media  ">
    <div class="container">
                <div class="row s-content-media__content-wrap  h-reverse-order--row ">

            <div class="col-12 col-md-5 fade slide-up  offset-lg-1  s-content-media__content-col  s-content-media__content-col--aligned-center ">

                <header class="c-header s-content-media__header">
                            
            <h2 class="c-heading c-header__heading heading-four ">
        Free-space coupling solution

    </h2>
            </header>

                
                <div class="c-content  s-content-media__content">
        <p>Single Quantum provides a free-space coupling solution, Iris X. It comprises a cryostat with an optical window, which can host an SNSPD array perfect for free-space QKD as well as deep-space laser communication, as demonstrated in <a href="https://www.esa.int/Enabling_Support/Operations/Shining_a_light_on_NASA_s_deep_space_demo?fbclid=IwAR1u_TSz9MoVV2Dspx7gQi0VyeMQMBKecOCJN4nVD7L03jGYVfT5efTvb5E" data-wpel-link="external" rel="external noopener noreferrer">our collaboration with ESA for Psyche Mission</a>.</p>
<ul class="list-one">
<li>Low noise environment for photon detection</li>
<li>Ample space allocated for auxiliary components</li>
<li>Capability to host an SNSPD array of up to 48 pixels</li>
</ul>
<p><a class="button-one" href="https://www.singlequantum.com/solutions/space-tech-line/" data-wpel-link="internal"><span class="button-link-label">Discover more</span></a></p>

    </div>

                
                
            </div>
            <div class="col-12 col-md-6   s-content-media__image--aligned-center  s-content-media__image">
                    
                <div class="c-media s-media__media c-media--has-popup ">
    
    
    
            
        <div class="c-image c-media__image s-media__image none ">
    <img width="1875" height="1440" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZpZXdCb3g9IjAgMCAxODc1IDE0NDAiIHdpZHRoPSIxODc1IiBoZWlnaHQ9IjE0NDAiIGRhdGEtdT0iJTJGd3AtY29udGVudCUyRnVwbG9hZHMlMkYyMDI0JTJGMTAlMkZNYXNrLWdyb3VwLTIuanBnIiBkYXRhLXc9IjE4NzUiIGRhdGEtaD0iMTQ0MCIgZGF0YS1iaXA9IiI+PC9zdmc+" data-spai="1" class="c-image__img " alt="" decoding="async" data-spai-target="src" data-spai-orig="/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/Mask-group-2.jpg" data-spai-exclude="none" data-spai-orig-obs="true">
  </div>

        
            
    
    </div>
            </div>
        </div>
    </div>
</section>
                    <section id="section-number-6" class="section-number-6 section--bg-color s-content-with-sidebar section--bottom-p-small">
    <div class="container">
        <div class="row">
            <div class="col-12 col-md-4 form--bg-dark sidebar fade slide-up">
                                                                                                                        <div class="c-content ">
        <h4>Datasheet</h4>
<p><a class="button-one" href="#"><span class="button-link-label">Download datasheet</span></a></p>

    </div>
                                                                                                                                                                                                                </div>
            <div class="col-12 col-md-7 content-right  fade slide-up">
                                                                                                        <div class="c-content ">
        <h5>How to keep your data safe with quantum technology?</h5>
<p>One of the most promising approach to ensure a secure communication is Quantum Key Distribution (QKD), a quantum cryptography method to produce and share encryption keys. Moreover, quantum internet can enable sending and receiving quantum bits (qubits) over a large network, paving the way to cloud quantum computing.</p>
<p>Here, single photon detection is the key enabling technology. In particular, Single Quantum SNSPDs prove to be ideal for QKD and quantum internet applications, due to their outstanding performance [1,2,3].</p>

    </div>
                                                                                                                                                                                                                        </div>
        </div>
    </div>
</section>
                    <section data-wpr-lazyrender="1" id="section-number-7" class="section-number-7 section--bg-color s-content-with-sidebar section--top-p-small section--bottom-p-small">
    <div class="container">
        <div class="row">
            <div class="col-12 col-md-4 form--bg-dark sidebar fade slide-up">
                                                                                                                        <div class="c-content ">
        <h4>Collaborations</h4>

    </div>
                                                                                                                                                                                                                </div>
            <div class="col-12 col-md-7 content-right  fade slide-up">
                                                                                                        <div class="c-content ">
        <h5><strong>Collaborating to advance quantum communication</strong></h5>
<p>At Single Quantum, we value collaboration as a cornerstone of innovation. By partnering with leading organizations and research groups, we bring together cutting-edge technologies to address the challenges of quantum communication. These collaborations enable us to push the boundaries of what’s possible, ensuring robust and scalable solutions for the growing demands of secure data transmission.</p>

    </div>
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    <div class="icons">
                            <div class="column-1">
                                                                        <div class="icon-row">

                                                                                            <div class="c-content ">
        <h5>Toshiba Europe and Single Quantum parnter to probide extended long-distance QKD deployment capability</h5>
<p>Toshiba Europe Ltd. and Single Quantum B.V. have collaborated to test and validate long-distance deployments of Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) technology.</p>
<p>Following extended validation testing of Toshiba’s QKD technology and Single Quantum’s superconducting nanowire single photon detectors (SNSPDs), both companies are pleased to announce a solution that substantially extends the transmission range for QKD deployment over fibre connections, up to and beyond 300km.</p>
<p><a class="button-one" href="/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/Toshiba-Single-Quantum-PR-release_final-19.04-1.pdf" data-wpel-link="internal"><span class="button-link-label">Read the press release</span></a></p>

    </div>
                                                                                                                                                                                                                        </div>
        </div>
    </div>
</section>

const revokeListeners = [];
window.addRevokeListener = (callback) => {
	revokeListeners.push(callback);
};
document.addEventListener("cmplz_revoke", function (e) {
	cmplz_set_cookie('cmplz_consent_mode', 'revoked', false );
	revokeListeners.forEach((callback) => {
		callback();
	});
});

const consentListeners = [];
/**
 * Called from GTM template to set callback to be executed when user consent is provided.
 * @param callback
 */
window.addConsentUpdateListener = (callback) => {
	consentListeners.push(callback);
};
document.addEventListener("cmplz_fire_categories", function (e) {
	var consentedCategories = e.detail.categories;
	const consent = {
		'security_storage': "granted",
		'functionality_storage': "granted",
		'personalization_storage':  cmplz_in_array( 'preferences', consentedCategories ) ? 'granted' : 'denied',
		'analytics_storage':  cmplz_in_array( 'statistics', consentedCategories ) ? 'granted' : 'denied',
		'ad_storage': cmplz_in_array( 'marketing', consentedCategories ) ? 'granted' : 'denied',
		'ad_user_data': cmplz_in_array( 'marketing', consentedCategories ) ? 'granted' : 'denied',
		'ad_personalization': cmplz_in_array( 'marketing', consentedCategories ) ? 'granted' : 'denied',
	};

	//don't use automatic prefixing, as the TM template needs to be sure it's cmplz_.
	let consented = [];
	for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(consent)) {
		if (value === 'granted') {
			consented.push(key);
		}
	}
	cmplz_set_cookie('cmplz_consent_mode', consented.join(','), false );
	consentListeners.forEach((callback) => {
		callback(consent);
	});
});
